text: The patient is positioned in lithotomy, and trochars are inserted for laparoscopic access. The surgeon begins the procedure with sharp dissection of adhesions, revealing a thickened peritoneal reflection indicative of chronic disease.

The patient is positioned in lithotomy, and trochars are inserted for laparoscopic access. The surgeon begins with sharp dissection of adhesions, revealing a thickened peritoneal reflection indicative of chronic disease.

The surgeon dissects the posterior aspect of the rectum, continuing the dissection laterally. The peritoneal reflection is opened anteriorly, and the dissection progresses through the pelvis until the levator muscles are visible.

The surgeon dissects the posterior of the rectum, continuing laterally and opening the peritoneal reflection on the anterior side. Dissection proceeds through the pelvis until the levator muscles are visible. A rectangular mesh is placed on the anterior surface of the rectum and fixed to the ventral wall with separate sutures. Spiral tackers secure the mesh to the promontorium. The peritoneum is closed to prevent intra-abdominal organs from contacting the mesh.

A rectangular mesh is positioned on the anterior surface of the rectum and secured to the ventral wall with separate sutures. Spiral tackers are used to fix the mesh to the promontory. The peritoneum is then closed to prevent intra-abdominal organs from contacting the mesh.
